summary: McKenzie provides a comprehensive playbook for micro-ISV developers looking to increase software sales beyond their initial launch. He covers seven key strategies: installing analytics, SEO optimization, link building through valuable content, blogging with evergreen content, eliminating checkout barriers, offering money-back guarantees, and optimizing AdWords campaigns. The advice is grounded in his own experience selling Bingo Card Creator and emphasizes writing content in the language customers actually use rather than marketing jargon. He stresses that improving sales is a long-term iterative process of testing and observation, not a quick fix.
key_points: Write content using the exact language your customers use in search queries rather than corporate marketing speak — matching their natural language is the key to SEO success
Create evergreen content that remains valuable for years rather than chasing trending topics; today's labor should pay dividends every day thereafter
Sites don't attract links — content attracts links; create genuinely useful resources for your niche that people want to share with colleagues
Eliminate every friction point in checkout: offer multiple payment processors, display credit card logos, deliver instantly, and make sure customers know the process is fast and easy
Improving sales is an iterative long-term process of testing and observation, not something you can accomplish in a single burst of effort
money_quotes: Congratulations on finishing an application that real people think was worth spending money on. That's an accomplishment. It is also the easiest part about running a software business.
Write about what your customers care about in language similar to what they use.
If you write your page like they write their search queries, you win.
Nobody writes 'new paradigm in best-of-breed B2C customer empowerment synergies' in Google!
Sites don't attract links. Content attracts links.
Writing evergreens is like investing in yourself — it is a way for today's labor to pay dividends tomorrow and every day thereafter.
You don't get the benefits of having your process be instant if you don't mention them to your customers before checkout!
Consistent sustained improvement is the key to long-term success.
key_insight: Increasing software sales is fundamentally about creating genuinely useful content in the language your customers actually use, then systematically removing every barrier between discovery and purchase.
